title: Leontodon × carreiroi
intro: Leontodon × carreiroi, also known as Carreiro's hawk's beard, is a hybrid plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers and jagged, toothed leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and support immune function. Bioactive constituents of Leontodon × carreiroi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leontodon × carreiroi is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Leontodon × carreiroi is a hybrid of the plant genus Leontodon. It is a rare species of plant that has been found in Portugal and Spain. The classification of Leontodon × carreiroi is as a hybrid between the species Leontodon hispidus and another species of Leontodon. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this species. The classification can be further verified by examining the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
